creator: ohartmann@walstatt.org
text:
From-To: freebsd-ports-bugs->clsung By: rm When: Wed Jun 6 09:29:13 UTC 2012 Why: Hi Oliver. It looks like textproc/refdb didn't working with php 5.4 and there is no new version available. You should try to revert to php 5.3 and check if it fixes the issue for you. There is also link that may be useful: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/8971261/php-5-4-call-time-pass-by-reference-easy-fix-available ---- Sorry for the crude reply (didn't found a suitable reply address). You're right, I checked with Google what is going on. Having the recent mess of the ports tree in mind, it seems unwise to me to fall back to the previous PHP version 5.3. What I did: Naively, I simply removed the ampersand (&) from the call causing the error. Funny, it works! I do not know what side effect this could cause, I'm not familiar with PHP. In C/C++ such a thing would cause trouble - im sure. In PHP - something works anyway. Regards, Oliver --- index.php.orig 2012-06-09 10:55:09.000000000 +0200 +++ index.php 2012-06-09 10:56:45.000000000 +0200 @@ -1528,7 +1528,7 @@ // Mapping CCL terms to RPN as defined above (bib1) for each search yaz_ccl_conf($id[$i], $bib1); // Parse input against yaz_ccl_conf; if error report message; defines cclresult as output of parsing - if (!yaz_ccl_parse($id[$i], $term, &$cclresult)) { + if (!yaz_ccl_parse($id[$i], $term, $cclresult)) { echo '

Error: ' . $cclresult["errorstring"] . '

Clear results

'; continue; }
